$comment: Schema audit performed 2026-05-30. PulseMCP's current submission form (https://www.pulsemcp.com/submit) accepts only a single URL — they then automate the rest. PulseMCP's API docs (https://www.pulsemcp.com/api) confirm two ingestion paths: (1) manual submission via the form's URL field, (2) automatic ingestion from the official MCP Registry at registry.modelcontextprotocol.io. The earlier draft's claim of 'scrapes npm for the mcp keyword' is not corroborated by their current docs — that path may have been retired in favor of the official-registry feed. The metadata below is the SOURCE CONTENT a maintainer can paste into the form's free-text fields (description / categories) if the form prompts for them, or use to write the curated entry once PulseMCP picks the listing up. Submit the npm registry listing for @peekdev/mcp as the URL.
automatic_ingest_note: PulseMCP ingests the official MCP Registry daily — if peek is published to registry.modelcontextprotocol.io first (see modelcontextprotocol-servers.md), PulseMCP should auto-discover it within ~24h. Check before submitting the form manually to avoid duplicates.
name: peek
short_description: Bring your real authenticated browser session to AI coding agents. Local-first MCP server + Chrome extension.
long_description:
peek lets AI coding assistants see (and optionally drive) the user's actual logged-in browser. A Chrome MV3 extension records the session (rrweb DOM + console + network metadata, with PII masking) and ships it through a native-messaging stdio bridge to a local MCP server (`peek-mcp`). All data stays on the user's machine — sessions are persisted to a local SQLite database at `~/.peek/sessions.db`. Per-origin opt-in, five permission levels (Off -> YOLO), and a destructive-action blocklist gate every action the agent can take.

Use cases: debugging a flaky login flow with Claude Code, asking Cursor to reproduce a bug a teammate saw, having Cline scaffold a Playwright test from a session you just recorded, letting Windsurf walk through an admin dashboard you have access to but the agent does not.

See the privacy policy at docs/peek/PRIVACY_POLICY.md.

install_instructions.extension: Install the companion Chrome extension via the Chrome Web Store (link added once the CWS listing is approved) — peek-mcp requires it to capture sessions.

quality_signals: Apache-2.0 OSS, public repo at https://github.com/Cubenest/rrweb-stack
Published privacy policy + threat model at docs/peek/PRIVACY_POLICY.md and docs/peek/THREATMODEL.md
Test suite: 198 mcp + 256 extension green at submission time; CI runs Playwright persistent-context smoke
No telemetry, no cloud — entirely local; verifiable from the source (no network destinations in `packages/peek-mcp/src/`)
TODO_AFTER_CWS: Once the Chrome Web Store listing is live, the long_description's 'extension' install line can be updated with the actual CWS URL.
PulseMCP listings can show a 'gallery image' (typically 1280x640 PNG); the CWS submission assets will satisfy this — re-use them once they're produced.
TODO_VERIFY: PulseMCP filters out 'low quality implementations' per their stats page. The quality_signals[] above are the maintainer's pitch — confirm they're surfaced in the listing once it lands.
If PulseMCP's auto-ingest from the MCP Registry already picked up peek before this manual submission, skip the form to avoid a duplicate. Check at https://www.pulsemcp.com/servers?q=peek first.
